statute: Maryland Code Business Regulation § 8-301 et seq. (Maryland Home Improvement Commission); MHIC

mandatory_clauses.label: MHIC license disclosure
mandatory_clauses.note: MISSING MHIC LICENSE NUMBER MAKES CONTRACT VOIDABLE BY THE HOMEOWNER.

mandatory_clauses.label: Salesperson registration (if applicable)
Right to cancel notice
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under Maryland law (Real Property Article § 9-101 et seq.), contractors, subcontractors, and materialmen who provide labor or materials to your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. You should obtain lien waivers from all subcontractors and suppliers before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Maryland Guaranty Fund notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
MARYLAND HOME IMPROVEMENT GUARANTY FUND

If you suffer a loss as the result of a home improvement transaction, you may be entitled to compensation from the Maryland Home Improvement Guaranty Fund. You must file a claim with the Maryland Home Improvement Commission. For information, contact MHIC at (410) 230-6309.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)

penalties_for_non_compliance: Missing MHIC license = contract VOIDABLE by homeowner. MHIC enforcement action. Guaranty Fund may compensate homeowners for contractor violations. Criminal penalties for unlicensed work.
notes: Maryland's MHIC licensing is strictly enforced. A missing MHIC license number makes the contract voidable at the homeowner's option — the homeowner can walk away and the contractor has no recourse. All home improvement work over $100 requires a written contract. If a salesperson sold or solicited the contract, their MHIC salesperson registration number must also appear. Warranty terms must be included in the contract. Cancel notice must be by certified mail or hand delivery.
